mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to generate a positive mean forward return over 60 days, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions relative to its 12-month average produce positive 5-day forward returns in over 60% of observed cases during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5% annualized indicates insufficient aggregate demand to sustain corporate profit growth, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth stays below the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate forces tighter financial conditions, which discounts future earnings and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and non-declining FEDFUNDS yields a positive mean 60-day forward return with statistical significance (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 250,000 per month during periods of high unemployment signals aggressive economic recovery, which boosts market sentiment and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS surges above 250k during high unemployment regimes fail to produce positive 5-day forward returns in at least 65% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production greater than 0.5% indicates immediate supply chain disruptions and reduced corporate output, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months where INDPRO drops below the 0.5% threshold.
